name: relationalai
documentation:
# The RelationalAI Python Library

The Python library for building and querying knowledge graphs with RelationalAI.

For more information, visit our documentation at [https://relational.ai/docs](https://relational.ai/docs).

functions.name: relationalai_test_util.snapshot.path_to_slug
documentation: Parameterize tests over all files in a directory

functions.name: relationalai_test_util.snapshot.validate_block_snapshots
documentation:
Execute the program at `file_path`, applying `get_snapshot_str` to all
blocks and ensuring the result has not changed since the last run.

functions.name: relationalai_test_util.snapshot.do_snapshot_test
documentation:
Execute the program at `file_path`, validating that query results
have not changed since the last run. Uses the given `config` and
`engine_size` if provided, or the ambient config if not.

classes.name: relationalai_test_util.snapshot.ISourceInfo
documentation: Tap block info out of the debug log

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.emit_literal
documentation: Emit `v` as its equivalent literal representation in rel.

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.emit_nested_relation
documentation: Emit a set of defs encoding `obj` in GNF on `prefix`.

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.sanitize_identifier
documentation: Return a string safe to use as a top level identifier in rel, such as a variable or relation name.

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.ignore_known_problems
documentation: Filter out issues already in `problems` when checking responses for rel problems.

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.assert_no_problems
documentation: Throw a vaguely-readable exception if rel problems were reported with the given transaction.

functions.name: relationalai.rel_utils.process_gnf_results
documentation: Process GNF results into a nested object keyed by the key(s) in `key_names`

functions.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.compute_file_hash
documentation: Digest the given file to a content-addressable hash.

functions.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.compute_str_hash
documentation: Digest the given string to a hash.

functions.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.read_resource_context
documentation: This could instead be a subclass of Context, it behaves exactly as if it was
but doing so happened to be larger and (seemingly?) more brittle due to all the

functions.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.import_file
documentation: Automatically load `source` into `model` using the most appropriate loader.

functions.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.emit_delete_import
documentation: Utility fn for emitting the rel to delete the specified import relation and all its metadata.

classes.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.ResourceProxy
documentation: Represents a (optimistically-assumed to be present) resource relation, such as a CSV.

classes.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.Loader

methods.name: relationalai.loaders.loader.Loader.load
documentation: Load a snapshot of the given source data into the given model.
Base class for objects that handle the particulars of mapping an import source to a resource relation.

functions.name: relationalai.errors.suggest
documentation: Format a list of suggestions, adapted to the current runtime environment.

functions.name: relationalai.errors.adapt_to_env
documentation: Adapt a suggestion to work in the current runtime environment if possible, or exclude it if not.

classes.name: relationalai.errors.InvalidVersionKind
documentation: whether the python library or the Rel schema is out of date (or both!)

classes.name: relationalai.errors.Errors
documentation: TODO: Take out call_source from Errors class and put it in the main module

classes.name: relationalai.loaders.csv.CSVLoader

methods.name: relationalai.loaders.csv.CSVLoader.guess_schema
documentation: Guess the schema by reading a chunk of the file.
Load CSV files and URLs in RAI DB using `load_csv`.

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.version_range
documentation: A simple container for 2 version objects, a lower bound and an upper bound, plus a version
Equivalent to a packaging SpecifierSet defined as ">={lower_bound}, <{upper_bound}", but
has the benefit that we can check whether a version that is not contained by the set is
lower than the lower bound or higher than the higher bound, which is important for error
The vrange string is the Rel package manager representation of the low-high range. It
differs from the python notation in that the upper bounds use wildcards and are inclusive

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.generate_query_package_manager
documentation: Generate a Rel query that will lookup the versions of Rel libraries currently installed in
the database, as well as whether the registry is up to date. This is used if the package

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.generate_query_version_check
documentation: Generate a Rel query that will just check the static dependency versions. This is used if
the package manager is not disabled.

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.check_package_manager
documentation: Analyze the response of the "query_package_manager" transaction.
Return a tuple of booleans. The first boolean represents whether the registry is outdated
and need to be updated. The second boolean represents whether the packages in the
database are outdated and need to be updated.
If `warn_on_packages` is set, also warn on outdated packages.

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.generate_update_registry
documentation: Generate a Rel query to update the package registry, i.e. update the metadata to ensure
the database is up to date with the available packages.

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies.generate_update_packages
documentation: Generate a Rel query to update the package dependencies in the database to the latest

functions.name: relationalai.dependencies._compare_dependencies
documentation: Compare the current packages (in lock) and the value of std::version (in std) against the
expected package_deps and static_deps. Raise warnings when necessary and return whether
we should make the package manager update the package versions.

functions.name: relationalai.tools.dev.watch
documentation: Watch a DIRECTORY and re-run a SCRIPT on file changes.

classes.name: relationalai.util.snowflake_handler.SnowflakeHandler
documentation:
This is a logging handler that inserts spans directly into a Snowflake table.

It uses a queue and a worker thread to buffer spans and then insert them in batches.

classes.name: relationalai.tools.cli_controls.Spinner
documentation:
Shows a spinner control while a task is running.
The finished_message will not be printed if there was an exception and the failed_message is provided.

functions.name: relationalai.util.spans_file_handler.maybe_add_spans_file_handler
documentation:
Add a SpansFileHandler to the logger if the 'ENABLE_SPANS_FILE_HANDLER' environment variable is set to True,
or if the 'if_not_set' parameter is True.

classes.name: relationalai.util.spans_file_handler.SpansFileHandler
documentation:
This logger writes spans to a local .jsonl file inside the `spans_output` folder as they finish.
The primary goal is to ingest these spans into Snowflake for analytics.

classes.name: relationalai.util.tracing_handler.TracingHandler
documentation: This logger prints spans to stdout as they start and finish.

functions.name: relationalai.util.span_format_test.assert_valid_span_structure
documentation:
Assert that the tree of spans & events has a form that will
work for the debuggers.

functions.name: relationalai.util.span_tracker.format_span_msg
documentation: region Span formatting and filtering functions

functions.name: relationalai.util.span_tracker._get_masked_config
documentation: Return a config with all sensitive keys (secrets and passwords) removed, such that it can be safely logged

classes.name: relationalai.util.span_tracker.SpanTracker
documentation:
Abstract base class for tracking spans produced as log messages. 
Provides methods to handle the start and end of spans, format span messages, and manage span attributes.

functions.name: relationalai.tools.cli.latest_version
documentation: Get the current version of a package on PyPI.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.util.scrub_url
documentation: replace the values of the URL parameters that start with X-Amz- with XXX

functions.name: relationalai.clients.util.poll_with_specified_overhead
documentation: @NOTE: `overhead_rate` should fall between 0.05 and 0.5 depending on how time sensitive / expensive the operation in question is.

modules.name: relationalai.clients
documentation: Azure import is dropped because we need to to do runtime import of azure module to support our package running on Snowflake Notebook

class_variables.name: relationalai.clients.types.ImportsStatus.info
documentation: @FIXME: This appears to be a stringified JSON (!?) object containing the same data as this dict + more

classes.name: relationalai.clients.profile_polling.ProfilePollerHandler
documentation:
A handler which sees transaction start events come over the log stream,
starts polling for profile events for each transaction, and puts them into
the same stream.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config._search_upwards_for_file
documentation:
Search for `file` in the current directory and all parent directories.

Returns the absolute path to the file if found, otherwise None.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config._search_userdir_for_file
documentation:
Search for `file` in the user's home directory.

Returns the absolute path to the file if found, otherwise None.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config._parse_and_map_config
documentation:
Parse the config file at `file` and return a dictionary of config values.

Handles both TOML and INI files.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config._get_full_config
documentation:
Returns a dictionary representing the props to be used for the currently active profile. Incorporates the following rules:
1. The `snowflake_connection` key fills in the properties from the Snowflake connections.toml file.
2. Profiles with the same name are merged in order of appearance, with earlier profiles taking precedence.
3. The profile indicated by the `active_profile` key is merged into other top-level properties.

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config._legacy_config_files
documentation:
Generates all legacy config files found

Includes both ~/.rai/config and rai.config (in a parent directory
or the user's home directory)

functions.name: relationalai.clients.config.save_config
documentation:
Convenience function to save a raiconfig.toml file with the given toml_string.

rai.save_config("""
auto_suspend_mins = 30
""")

classes.name: relationalai.clients.config.ConfigFile

methods.name: relationalai.clients.config.ConfigFile.map
documentation: Apply a function `f` to all props dictionaries in the configuration files, including the top-level dictionary and all profiles.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.config.ConfigFile.merge
documentation:
Merge the profiles from `other` into this ConfigFile object.
For common keys, the values from `self` take precedence.

Represents of a file that contains some config information, including both raiconfig.toml and rai.config files.

Used to handle collection and merging of configuration properties from multiple sources.

classes.name: relationalai.clients.config.ConfigStore
documentation: Interface to the project raiconfig.toml file -- used to read and write configuration properties.

classes.name: relationalai.clients.config.Config
documentation: Represents an active collection of configuration properties.

functions.name: relationalai.dsl.to_var
documentation: @FIXME: This should only return a Var or None but that's gonna take some doing.

classes.name: relationalai.dsl.Type
documentation: @NOTE: `omit_intrinsic_type_in_hash` exists to keep node hashes (which are namespaced by their graph) stable
when new nodes are directly created via `Node.add(x = ..)` instead of marking existing entities as
nodes. Possible hash collisions between types happen to be okay in this case because of the access
paths, but it's _not_ generally safe to use it otherwise.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources._check_exec_async_status
documentation: Check whether the given transaction has completed.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources.decrypt_stream
documentation: Decrypt the provided stream with PKCS#5 padding handling.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources._decrypt_artifact
documentation: Decrypts the artifact data using provided encryption material.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources._list_exec_async_artifacts
documentation: Grab the list of artifacts produced in the transaction and the URLs to retrieve their contents.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources._fetch_exec_async_artifacts
documentation: Grab the contents of the given artifacts from SF in parallel using threads.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.Resources._parse_exec_async_results
documentation: Mimics the logic in _parse_arrow_results of railib/api.py#L303 without requiring a wrapping multipart form.

methods.name: relationalai.clients.snowflake.SnowflakeClient.poll_use_index
documentation: if data is ready, break the loop
if data is not ready, print the status of the tables or engines
if data is not ready and there are errors, collect the errors and raise exceptions
